subsections.title: Iteration 1
subsections.author: Aaron
subsections.content: In our first iteration of the drivetrain code, we implemented basic mecanum control logic using field-centric navigation. The primary goal was to ensure smooth strafing and consistent odometry tracking. We utilized a custom PID controller for rotational stability, which significantly reduced drift during high-speed translation. Moving forward, we plan to tune the feedforward constants to account for the chassis' weight distribution.

subsections.title: Prototyping Phase
subsections.author: Sarah
subsections.content: Initial tests with compliant wheels showed promise, but we encountered slipping when acquiring elements from off-angles. We tested different durometer wheels (35A vs 50A) and found that a mixed setup provides the best grip-to-slip ratio. The intake geometry was adjusted to a 30-degree approach angle, reducing the time required to securely grab a pixel by 40%.
